source_jobs.title: Sales Development Representative
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT PAYZEN

PayZen is on a mission to bring financial health to healthcare. Our products improve both access and affordability for patients and cash flow & administrative complexity for medical providers. We are trusted by a rapidly growing number of health systems, hospitals, and physician groups and are backed by leading venture capital investors, including NEA, SignalFire, Viola Group, and 7Wire Ventures.




ABOUT THE ROLE

PayZen is seeking a motivated Sales Development Representative with a demonstrated track record of top performance, a passion for technology, and the drive to build an incredible sales career. Your primary responsibility will be to help turn prospects and leads into qualified opportunities by partnering with Marketing and Sales to create and execute on program and outbound campaigns.



This position requires heavy outbound calling, email sending, social media research, consultative selling skills, and tight collaboration with the Account Executives to generate new opportunities. The SDR will be expected to report on qualifications and call activity and stay thoroughly informed on PayZen’s platform and the Healthcare Fintech industry in general.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Develop opportunities for the sales team from prospecting and marketing-generated leads

 - Quickly learn the PayZen product, effectively communicate the value proposition, and be able to react to objections and competitive questions

 - Research accounts, identify key players, generate interest, and develop accounts to stimulate opportunities

 - Develop and execute on a strong prospecting plan of attack, including email and call scripts

 - Interact with prospects via telephone and email with a high attention to detail

 - Set qualified discovery calls for the appropriate AE, educating rep as necessary about the opportunity

 - Play a critical role in the growth of our organization by consistently achieving qualified opportunity quotas

 - Track all relevant qualification and lead management activity using Salesforce




WHAT WE TYPICALLY LOOK FOR

 - 2+ years sales experience in a fast-paced technology company

 - Experience using Salesforce.com or comparable CRM, Outreach.io, Zoominfo and LinkedIn Sales Navigator

 - Strong understanding of lead development best practices

 - A track record of high achievement in current and previous roles hitting or exceeding quotas

 - A builder’s mentality and tendency to thrive in collaborative environments

 - A strong drive, work ethic, and results-driven operating style

 - Resourcefulness and a positive can-do attitude

 - Excitement about continuously improving your skills and impact

 - Experience using Outreach, Salesloft, or Salesforce High Velocity Sales/Sales Engagement is a strong plus

 - Experience in the Healthcare and/or Healthcare Finance industries is a strong plus




WHY PAYZEN?

 - An opportunity to positively affect the lives of thousands of patients.

 - A chance to join a high-growth company at an early stage.

 - Contribute to the development of a product that revolutionizes medical payments.

 - Become a key member of a collaborative, high-performing team.

 - Competitive pay, benefits, and stock options.

source_jobs.title: Strategic Account Executive - West
source_jobs.description:
Please note that this is a pipeline requisition; while we may not have an immediate seat open today, we are continuously reviewing applications to build our roster for upcoming territory expansions.



About PayZen

PayZen is on a mission to bring financial health to healthcare. Our products improve both access and affordability for patients and cash flow & administrative complexity for medical providers. We are trusted by a rapidly growing number of health systems, hospitals, and physician groups and are backed by leading venture capital investors, including NEA, SignalFire, Viola Group, and 7Wire Ventures.

About the Role

As we continue our expansion into the nation's most complex health systems, we are continuously looking to connect with strategic and results-oriented Account Executives. We’re building the operating system for the next trillion dollars in healthcare payments—creating a world where patients can transact autonomously and securely across real-world systems.

We are always looking for people who match the ambition of this mission. When you join the Sales Team, you will collaborate closely with Provider Success, Marketing, and Implementation to drive massive growth. This is a US Remote role.

 

What You'll Do

 - Ownership of an assigned territory focused on closing new logos

 - Selling into large health systems and complex environments

 - Source and develop new pipeline opportunities working closely with Marketing & Demand Generation

 - Articulate the value prop of PayZen’s affordability suite to target accounts

 - Collaborate with the analytics and implementation teams to deliver an account specific proposal and customized ROIs to prospective clients

 - Tightly manage the enterprise account decision making processes with multiple stakeholders (different functions & levels of seniority)

 - Deliver clear and realistic updates on pipeline forecast as an input for planning

 - Travel, when appropriate, for client visits, presentations, and events

 

What We're Looking For

 - 6+ years of experience selling enterprise SaaS solutions, specifically to hospital systems and physician groups.

 - Strategic Mindset: Proven ability to solve unique problems and develop creative approaches to achieve organizational goals.

 - Industry Expertise: Prior experience selling Revenue Cycle Management (RCM) solutions is a strong plus.

 - Executive Presence: The gravitas and communication skills necessary to engage and influence C-suite executives at large enterprises.

 - High Agency: A self-starter who thrives in a dynamic startup environment and possesses a "builder's mentality".

 - Outcome Oriented: A track record of achieving and exceeding quotas in high-growth technology companies.

 

Why PayZen?

 - An opportunity to positively affect the lives of thousands of patients.

 - A chance to join a high-growth company at an early stage.

 - Contribute to the development of a product that revolutionizes medical payments.

 - Become a key member of a collaborative, high-performing team.

 - Competitive pay, benefits, and stock options.

Please note that this is a pipeline requisition; while we may not have an immediate seat open today, we are continuously reviewing applications to build our roster for upcoming territory expansions.




ABOUT PAYZEN

PayZen is on a mission to bring financial health to healthcare. Our products improve both access and affordability for patients and cash flow & administrative complexity for medical providers. We are trusted by a rapidly growing number of health systems, hospitals, and physician groups and are backed by leading venture capital investors, including NEA, SignalFire, Viola Group, and 7Wire Ventures.




ABOUT THE ROLE

As we continue our expansion into the nation's most complex health systems, we are continuously looking to connect with strategic and results-oriented Account Executives. We’re building the operating system for the next trillion dollars in healthcare payments—creating a world where patients can transact autonomously and securely across real-world systems.

We are always looking for people who match the ambition of this mission. When you join the Sales Team, you will collaborate closely with Provider Success, Marketing, and Implementation to drive massive growth. This is a US Remote role.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own a territory: Focus exclusively on closing new logos within health systems and physician groups with >$100M in revenue.

 - Drive the mission: Identify and manage complex enterprise sales cycles from initial discovery to close.

 - Articulate value: Deliver account-specific proposals and customized ROIs that demonstrate how PayZen revolutionizes patient affordability.

 - Navigate complexity: Manage multi-stakeholder decision processes across different functions and seniority levels.

 - Strategic forecasting: Provide clear, data-driven pipeline updates that inform company-wide planning and growth.

 - Collaborate cross-functionally: Work alongside Analytics and Implementation to ensure a seamless transition from prospect to long-term partner.




WHAT WE TYPICALLY LOOK FOR

 - 6+ years of experience selling enterprise SaaS solutions, specifically to hospital systems and physician groups.

 - Strategic Mindset: Proven ability to solve unique problems and develop creative approaches to achieve organizational goals.

 - Industry Expertise: Prior experience selling Revenue Cycle Management (RCM) solutions is a strong plus.

 - Executive Presence: The gravitas and communication skills necessary to engage and influence C-suite executives at large enterprises.

 - High Agency: A self-starter who thrives in a dynamic startup environment and possesses a "builder's mentality".

 - Outcome Oriented: A track record of achieving and exceeding quotas in high-growth technology companies.

source_jobs.title: Senior Strategic Finance Manager
source_jobs.description:
About PayZen

PayZen is on a mission to bring financial health to healthcare. Our products improve both access and affordability for patients and cash flow and administrative complexity for medical providers. We are trusted by a rapidly growing number of health systems, hospitals, and physician groups and are backed by leading venture capital investors.



About the role

The Strategic Finance Manager will contribute to a diverse portfolio of strategic, data-driven initiatives to scale our operations and optimize financial performance. This role will play a critical part in influencing decision-making processes across the company, driving operational excellence and shaping the financial trajectory of our company.

The ideal candidate will embody a proactive and growth-oriented mindset, demonstrating high levels of self-motivation, analytical ability, and have a keen focus on driving results.

If you have a passion for problem-solving, are a collaborative team player, and possess a strong desire to help shape the future of a fast-growing startup, we would love to hear from you.

The role is based in our San Francisco headquarters and hybrid (in office Monday, Tuesday, and Thursday).



What you will do

 - Own three-statement financial modeling, monthly reporting cycles, and the scalable data architecture that provides actionable insights for executive leadership

 - Design AI agent workflows to automate repeatable finance tasks—such as revenue forecasting and variance analysis—increasing team capacity and precision.

 - Lead annual and long-range planning processes, translating corporate objectives into rigorous financial goals and business plans.

 - Develop frameworks to analyze business trends and identify the key performance metrics that influence company-wide decision-making.

 - Drive the analytical support and execution for equity and debt fundraising initiatives to ensure long-term growth and IPO readiness.

 - Manage external financial reporting and strategic analysis for board meetings and investor inquiries.

 - Serve as a trusted partner to senior stakeholders in GTM, Product, Legal, and other parts of the organization on financial planning, pricing strategies, and resource allocation.
   

To thrive in this role, you have

 - 7+ years of experience in investment banking, private equity, or strategic finance at a high-growth technology startup.

 - Exceptional financial modeling skills combined with a practical ability to build or configure automations (no-code or code) and integrate AI agents into financial data flows.

 - Demonstrated experience integrating AI into complex workflows (Cowork and Claude Code preferred).

 - Proven track record of owning outcomes with defined KPIs and making data-driven decisions in high-stakes environments.

 - Strong data visualization skills, with a continuous drive to seek process improvements and automate data quality checks (SQL a plus!).

 - Excellent written and verbal communication skills with the ability to interact regularly with executive management and influence strategic policy.



Who you will work with:

 - Florence, FP&A Lead https://www.linkedin.com/in/florence-kong/ — built the model stack from scratch and will be your closest collaborator on data infrastructure. Ask her about trying new restaurants and wine in SF and you won't be able to get her to stop talking.

 - Robin Poore, Head of Capital Markets https://www.linkedin.com/in/robinpoore/— former Bluevine, owns all debt facility relationships. Equal parts spreadsheet and relationship guy — he knows every lender by first name. Robin can always be counted on to make sure leftover lunches don't go to waste and tracks every dollar moving around the firm.

 - Ken Brause, CFO https://www.linkedin.com/in/kenbrause/ — sets the strategic finance agenda you'll help execute. The kind of leader who explains the "why" before the "what." Ask him about his dog, Bailey!



What we offer

 - An opportunity to positively affect

notes: No tech roles posted, high volume of sales/account executive roles
